title: salvia amethystina subsp. vetasiana
intro: Salvia amethystina subsp. vetasiana, commonly known as purple-flowered sage, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae) that is native to the mountains of Central America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and aiding in digestion, although more research is needed to confirm these claims. Traditionally, the leaves and flowers of Salvia amethystina subsp. vetasiana are used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
